---
title: "About Larnix"
---

## The goal

Larnix spans 17 modules — from "what is AI" through classical machine learning,
deep learning, transformers, large language models, retrieval and agents,
MLOps, responsible AI, and a build-your-own-LLM capstone. Every chapter ships
runnable code; every module ends in graded exercises and a build-something
capstone.

The guiding promise: a learner with a low-spec laptop can complete the core of
the course for ₹0 — in-browser Python for the early modules, free Colab and
Kaggle tiers for the GPU modules, and a free local model fallback wherever a
lesson touches an API.

## How it is built

Larnix is a docs-as-code project. Chapters are plain-text Quarto and Jupyter
files; the site is statically generated and continuously checked: every code
cell executes in CI, prose is linted, links and spelling are verified, and a set
of "currency" gates keep fast-moving content honest.

## Status

This is the Phase P0 scaffold — the platform itself, proven end-to-end on a
single sample chapter before any module is written at scale. The build plan
lives in the project roadmap.
